api: Customer.io App API v1 (https://api.customer.io/v1, or https://api-eu.customer.io/v1 for the EU region)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every one of the 159 method+path operations in the real Journeys App API OpenAPI spec (journeys-app.json) is accounted for below, XOR'd between covered_by (16 streams + 10 writes this bundle now implements) and excluded (with a real closed-vocabulary category+reason). Categories used: duplicate_of (a per-record detail/sub-resource already reachable via a covered list stream, no bulk variant, and no parent-id-list to fan_out from), out_of_scope (a genuinely distinct product surface -- asset/design-studio CMS, ESP suppression administration, async export/import jobs, other send channels, data-dictionary config -- or a body shape (oneOf-discriminated, bulk-array-wrapped) the dialect cannot express as a single flat write action).

excluded.reason: file/image asset library management (upload, folder tree) -- a media-library CMS surface, not campaign/customer/reporting data; out of scope for this connector's read/write scope

creates a new liquid-data collection from an inline data array OR a hosted-file URL (oneOf request body); the dialect's write body construction has no oneOf-discriminated body shape, and the primary practical use (large tabular reference data upload) is a bulk-data-loading operation, not a small-record CRM/reporting mutation this connector's write scope targets

replaces an entire collection's data rows in one request (bulk tabular data replacement) -- same bulk-data-loading rationale as create above, not a small-record mutation

single-customer lookup requiring a specific email as a REQUIRED query param (not a bulk list) -- there is no list-all-customers endpoint in the App API by design (person/profile data is meant to be looked up by identifier, not paginated wholesale); no declarative list stream can be built from a lookup-only endpoint

bulk customer lookup by a list of ids in the POST body -- a batch-lookup surface, not a syncable list stream

kicks off an asynchronous bulk-export JOB (people or deliveries data dump); the exports stream already reads the resulting job metadata list, but starting a new export job is an async-job-creation action outside this connector's flat-record write scope

per-export detail/download sub-resource, keyed by a single export_id already enumerated by the exports stream; download in particular returns a binary/zip payload
kicks off an asynchronous bulk people-IMPORT job from an uploaded file URL -- an async bulk-load action, not a flat-record write

creates a new newsletter; body is a 6-way oneOf discriminated by message channel type (email/sms/push/in_app/webhook/inbox), each with a different required-field shape -- the dialect's single flat record_schema per write action has no oneOf/discriminator primitive to express six mutually-exclusive body shapes under one action name
per-newsletter detail, keyed by a single newsletter_id already enumerated by the newsletters stream

permanently deletes a newsletter and all its variants/metrics; omitted pending product-owner sign-off on adding a destructive per-object delete beyond the two already-covered destructive deletes (delete_manual_segment/delete_reporting_webhook) -- flaggable for a future increment, not a dialect limitation
per-newsletter variant-content list, keyed by a single newsletter_id
per-newsletter-variant detail, keyed by newsletter_id+content_id
updates a specific newsletter language/test variant's subject/body content -- content-authoring mutation, a CMS-shaped write distinct from this connector's flat-record CRM/reporting mutations
per-newsletter-variant metrics, keyed by newsletter_id+content_id
per-newsletter-variant link-click metrics, keyed by newsletter_id+content_id
creates a new language variant of a newsletter's content -- content-authoring mutation, not a flat-record CRM/reporting write
per-newsletter-language-variant detail, keyed by newsletter_id+language
updates a newsletter's language-variant content -- content-authoring mutation
deletes a newsletter's language variant -- content-authoring mutation, omitted pending product sign-off on adding it as a destructive action
per-newsletter delivery-message list, keyed by a single newsletter_id
per-newsletter aggregate metrics, keyed by a single newsletter_id
per-newsletter link-click metrics, keyed by a single newsletter_id
schedules a newsletter to send at a future time -- a send-lifecycle action tightly coupled to Design Studio content authoring, out of scope alongside it

sends a newsletter immediately -- a send-lifecycle action; sends real messages to a newsletter's full recipient list with no dry-run, higher blast-radius than the already-covered trigger_broadcast (which targets one specific broadcast_id's configured audience) and design_studio-adjacent content authoring
creates a language variant for an A/B test group -- content-authoring mutation
per-test-group-language-variant detail, keyed by newsletter_id+test_group_id+language
updates an A/B test group's language variant -- content-authoring mutation
deletes an A/B test group's language variant -- content-authoring mutation
per-newsletter A/B test-group list, keyed by a single newsletter_id
creates an A/B test group on a newsletter -- content-authoring/experiment-configuration mutation
bulk custom-object lookup by filter/query in the POST body -- a query/filter surface (capabilities.query is false), not a syncable list stream
per-object-instance attribute detail, keyed by object_type_id+object_id with no bulk list-all-instances endpoint documented for a given object_type_id
per-object-instance relationship detail, keyed by object_type_id+object_id, same no-bulk-list limitation
per-webhook detail, keyed by a single webhook_id already enumerated by the reporting_webhooks stream
per-segment detail, keyed by a single segment_id already enumerated by the segments stream
per-segment membership count, keyed by a single segment_id
per-segment membership list, keyed by a single segment_id
per-segment dependency ("what references this segment") lookup, keyed by a single segment_id

sends a live transactional in-app message; this connector's send-message write scope covers send_email only (the most broadly applicable channel) for wave2-Pass-B breadth -- each of these four channels has its own distinct required-field shape (device tokens, phone numbers, in-app message targeting) and can be added in a future increment without an engine change

sends a live transactional inbox message; this connector's send-message write scope covers send_email only (the most broadly applicable channel) for wave2-Pass-B breadth -- each of these four channels has its own distinct required-field shape (device tokens, phone numbers, in-app message targeting) and can be added in a future increment without an engine change

sends a live transactional push message; this connector's send-message write scope covers send_email only (the most broadly applicable channel) for wave2-Pass-B breadth -- each of these four channels has its own distinct required-field shape (device tokens, phone numbers, in-app message targeting) and can be added in a future increment without an engine change

sends a live transactional SMS message; this connector's send-message write scope covers send_email only (the most broadly applicable channel) for wave2-Pass-B breadth -- each of these four channels has its own distinct required-field shape (device tokens, phone numbers, in-app message targeting) and can be added in a future increment without an engine change

per-sender-identity dependency lookup, keyed by a single sender_id

mints a signed subscription-preferences-center token for one specific customer_id, for embedding in an outbound email link -- a per-request token-generation action, not catalog data or a flat-record mutation
